rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type scopes when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Use visible feature names and declared constants in AGREE contracts to ensure type alignment and syntactic validity.
generic_rules: Formulate guarantees as boolean expressions that relate visible variables to their expected ranges or conditions.
Ensure that all AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of the referenced features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use the pattern `eq variable : bool = expression` for defining range checks in AGREE contracts.
Ensure that all expressions in AGREE contracts match the expected types (bool, int, real) to avoid validator errors.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings or subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless explicitly represented through variables or outputs.
Do not define range assumptions or guarantees without concrete bounds or visible feature names.
